pattern: Reduce each word to its sequence of adjacent letter gaps, then the answer is the single word whose gap signature is unique among the group.
hints: Two words share a signature if their consecutive letter differences match position by position.
Compute a tuple of gaps for every word and group words by that tuple.
Exactly one tuple appears once; return the word that produced it.

edge_inputs.description: given example, odd one at the end
given example, odd one in the middle
odd one is the first word

stress.description: many words sharing one random gap pattern with a single planted outlier
stress.generator_python:
def gen(rng, size):
    n = max(3, size)
    L = rng.randint(2, 20)
    def random_gaps():
        # choose gaps and a start so all characters stay within [0,25]
        while True:
            gs = [rng.randint(-3, 3) for _ in range(L - 1)]
            prefix = [0]
            for g in gs:
                prefix.append(prefix[-1] + g)
            lo, hi = min(prefix), max(prefix)
            span = hi - lo
            if span <= 25:
                start = rng.randint(-lo, 25 - hi)
                return gs, start
    gaps, start = random_gaps()
    def build(gs, st):
        cur = st
        chars = [cur]
        for g in gs:
            cur += g
            chars.append(cur)
        return ''.join(chr(ord('a') + c) for c in chars)
    words = []
    for _ in range(n - 1):
        # vary the start but keep the same gap signature; reshift if out of range
        prefix = [0]
        for g in gaps:
            prefix.append(prefix[-1] + g)
        lo, hi = min(prefix), max(prefix)
        st = rng.randint(-lo, 25 - hi)
        words.append(build(gaps, st))
    odd_gaps, odd_start = random_gaps()
    while odd_gaps == gaps:
        odd_gaps, odd_start = random_gaps()
    pos = rng.randrange(n)
    words.insert(pos, build(odd_gaps, odd_start))
    return [words]
stress.note: all non-outlier words share one gap signature (varied starts); the outlier uses a distinct signature
